title: Monoon elongatum
intro: Monoon elongatum, also known as the "Malaysian tree", is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Monoon elongatum include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to possess significant cytotoxic activity against various types of cancer cells. The most common herbal preparations made with Monoon elongatum include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health problems.
